name: Daniel Jones
education: Master of Science (M.S.), Computer Science from Georgia Institute of Technology; Bachelors of Science, Computer Science from Rowan University
headline: Software Engineer @Xbox | TEDx Speaker | Tech Enthusiast | Maker | Adventurer | Mentor | Polymath
about:
As a software engineer at Microsoft, I work on the Xbox platform, creating engaging and immersive experiences for millions of gamers around the world. I use my expertise in React, C++, .NET, Azure, and other cutting-edge technologies to deliver scalable, secure, and high-performance solutions that meet the needs and expectations of our customers and partners.

As a technical leader and Sr. SWE with over 19 years of diverse experience, I have spanned many various industries, including finance, defense, gaming and telecommunications, just to name a few. I have led and contributed to projects building world-class cloud solutions, high-precision hardware monitoring systems, and real-time data ingestion and analysis. Recently, I've even worked with the Minecraft Launcher team when helping to improve the Xbox Commerce GDK. I enjoy being creative, collaborative, and staying curious. I am constantly seeking to learn new skills on my journey to solve complex problems that create value. My passion for mentoring has allowed me to payback so many others, both in and out of Microsoft.

Personal Philosophy: Every day I have the opportunity to discover excellence through play and curiosity. To persevere through adversity and build something amazing.

experiences.title: Senior Consultant (App Dev)
experiences.jobDescription: Working face-to-face with industry leading customers building world class cloud solutions with a diverse stack of technologies. I mentor and provide technical leadership both at Microsoft and on customer sites.

experiences.title: Senior Software Engineer
experiences.jobDescription: Technical lead overseeing two of our flagship commercial products, reporting directly to the CTO. These are high precision hardware monitoring systems used, by the US military, to monitor real-time sensor data and are responsible for approximately 80% of the annual company revenue. Built using C++ and .NET technologies, with SQL + MongoDB datastores. Real-time message ingestion, with buffering, is used to handle the large quantity of inbound data.

experiences.title: Senior Software Engineer
experiences.jobDescription: Senior Software Engineer working with nascent technologies to build radio frequency monitoring and signal origination software. This is a cleared facility conducting research and development for customers in the Signal Intelligenceindustry. Innovating in a cleared environment is challenging due to the sensitive nature of the content.

educations.title: Rowan University
educations.subtitle: Bachelors of Science, Computer Science
educations.description: Specializations: Information Technology and Artificial Intelligence

volunteerAndAwards.description: Work as a software mentor to two local high school FIRST Robotics programs. We encourage and foster growth for students interested in Science, Mathematics and Engineering disciplines. This has been a very rewarding experience for me and I hope to keep with it for many years to come.

publications.title: Pioneering Entrepreneurial Software Engineers
publications.source: 35th Annual ASEE/IEEE Frontiers in Education Conference

recommendationsReceived.name: Melissa Dietz-Rosso
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Senior Software Engineer at Microsoft
recommendationsReceived.context: Melissa worked with Daniel on the same team
recommendationsReceived.description:
Dan, as a technical lead on our project at ABC, you are the driving force for our effective collaboration and strive towards technical excellence. You inspire our growth and collaboration through clear, transparent, effective, and mindful communication. Whenever an issue arises, you're first on the scene to help developers debug and unblock themselves and follow up with the rest of the team to ensure no one else is stuck by the same issue. Your leadership and team first attitude is a driver of our success. 

I think you are a great mentor and role model, especially because you demonstrate a growth mindset and are positively self-aware. You welcome feedback and make space for others to improve upon or disagree with your approach, which fosters a collaborative environment where we are more open to speaking up and finding clear solutions that otherwise may have been ignored. 

You are also doing great work in the community through the work group advocacy with Anthony/Dona, leading technical mentor pods for Diana, driving MS Poll participation, and even volunteering with TEALS. You are a selfless leader who creates a space that allows others to bring their best self each day.

I value your honesty, authenticity, and positive can do attitude. As your mentee, I appreciate the way you deliver honest feedback to me, both technically and otherwise. I've learned so much from you and you are always thinking of new ways to teach me valuable skills. You encourage me to think creatively and contribute in ways that I had not thought of before!

recommendationsReceived.name: Lee Parris
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Technical Writer at DXC Technology
recommendationsReceived.context: Lee worked with Daniel on the same team
recommendationsReceived.description: Dan was a great teammate and mentor, and his technical skills were always sharp. He raised the bar for all the developers he worked with, always providing guidance on best practices and code efficiency.  The projects I saw him implement were well designed and stable.  Long after he moved on from our team, I still found myself saying "What would Dan do?", and used that to make technical adjustments as I tried to uphold the standards he had set for us.  Dan's conscientious work ethic would make him a great addition to any organization.

recommendationsReceived.name: Laura Riordan
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Relationship Architect, specializing in Technology Sales, Consulting, and IT Staffing. Dedicated to curating extraordinary and unforgettable customer experiences.
recommendationsReceived.description: Dan is one of those diamonds in the rough in the world of IT! He has that rare blend of technical expertise, excellent communication and an outgoing personality. Dan originally came highly recommended to me by one of his colleagues for a Senior .Net, contract to hire role, with our client, Bank of America. Dan's performance on the contract portion was exceptional and he was brought on as a full time employee where he continued to prove his value through both his technical and people skills. Dan is a pleasure to work and has always been open to providing advice as well as referrals when I have open positions. I highly recommend Dan for any Senior Developer or Architect roles.

recommendationsReceived.name: Hussein Syed Mohammed
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Vice President & General Manager I Vice President Product Management | Industrial & Instrumentation | GTM, Commercialization & Operations | Product Strategy | Product Portfolio Management | B2B & B2C | SaaS | AI | IIoT
recommendationsReceived.description: Daniel is an outstanding colleague with excellent work ethics. I managed to have the opportunity to work closely with him on several projects and can attest for his thirst for excellence. He is very sharp and technical, and most importantly has a kind heart and genuine interest in helping others. He is a good leader and team player who is willing to look at problems from multiple angles before deciding on a final solution. I believe that it is this certain quality that sets him out from many of his peers. His team won the second prize in Rowan University’s Software Development Competition and received attention from the Mayor of Glassboro’s office. The software was developed to help lower income and underprivileged families get financial aid for residential housing. With his strong technical background and ability to speak well in both professional and candid situations, he should be an asset to any team or company looking for a leader.

recommendations.name: Sherri Babylon
recommendations.subtitle: Fractional CAIO/CTO | AI only when it pays | Ex-Microsoft
recommendations.context: Daniel worked with Sherri on the same team
recommendations.description:
I've had the privilege of working alongside Sherri at Microsoft, and without hesitation, I can say she’s one of the most competent and reliable professionals I’ve ever partnered with. Sherri brings a rare combination of sharp analytical thinking, operational discipline, and calm leadership that makes even the most complex projects feel manageable.

Her ability to break down problems, prioritize effectively, and drive toward solutions, whether tactical or strategic, is second to none. She has a deep instinct for when to pivot between short-term fixes and long-term investments, always with the customer’s needs at the center of her decisions.

Sherri's background is as impressive as her presence in a room. With a strong foundation in project management and technical execution, she brings a wealth of experience to every initiative. Her work is anchored in three core pillars: operational readiness, process and platform modernization, and customer-centric technology strategy. I’ve seen her apply these pillars time and again to deliver measurable impact and lasting value.

If you’re looking for a leader who brings structure to chaos, clarity to ambiguity, and vision to execution; Sherri is exactly that person. She’s not just a trusted colleague; she’s the kind of partner who makes everyone around her better.

recommendations.name: Kyle Niver
recommendations.subtitle: Experienced Talent Acquisition/Recruiter/Human Resources - 10+ years Recruiting, Executive Search in MedTech, Manufacturing, Operations
recommendations.description: Kyle was hands down one of the best recruiters I've ever worked with. His communication, professionalism and dedication to details should be a model to all the other recruiters out there. As a software engineer you talk to a ton, I mean a literal ton, of recruiters. rarely do you find one of Kyle's caliber. This particular instance didn't pan out for me due to timing issues however I look forward to working with Kyle again in the future.

recommendations.name: Laura Riordan
recommendations.subtitle: Relationship Architect, specializing in Technology Sales, Consulting, and IT Staffing. Dedicated to curating extraordinary and unforgettable customer experiences.
recommendations.description: From my experience Laura is one of the most respectable recruiters I've ever had the pleasure of working with. Not only is she genuine and passionate about her job, but she is also incredibly well versed in many different types of technology and technical roles within an organization. I plan to work with Laura whenever possible in the future and I have recommended her to many of my developer colleagues.

recommendations.name: Andrew Van Slaars
recommendations.subtitle: Architect and technology leader focused on delivering better UX at scale.
recommendations.context: Daniel worked with Andrew on the same team
recommendations.description: Working with Andrew has been a real pleasure. His knack for quick decisive answers never ceases to amaze me. Andrew has always made himself available when it comes to work. He is never too busy to answer questions and give his professional opinion. I feel this is attributed to his keen use of time management and an ability to work well within a team. Andrew is a wealth of knowledge and I would always be willing to work along side him.

summary: Daniel Jones is currently a Microsoft professional who combines enterprise AI and product expertise with active grassroots community building in cybersecurity and developer communities. His posts regularly promote and organize regional events (notably BSides South Jersey), call for speakers and sponsors, and thank corporate and community partners — showing a knack for event operations and partnership development. He writes with a mix of practical advice (lists, how-tos, and career tips), thought leadership on responsible AI and enterprise tooling (Microsoft 365 Copilot, Copilot Agents, LangChain/Azure partnerships), and personal/mentorship-oriented reflections (burnout prevention, career outreach to students and alumni). Technical depth appears intermittently: he shares research-grade work (a graph change-point detection paper using martingales and Shapley attribution) alongside accessible guidance about DevOps, cloud security, and hands-on learning (CTFs, Hack The Box). The tone is professional, encouraging, and action-focused — blending promotional announcements, community calls-to-action, and concise technical commentary. Overall, his unique value is bridging large-enterprise AI/security perspectives with local community engagement, student mentoring, and practical, deployable guidance.
highlights: Cybersecurity Community Builder & Event Organizer (BSides South Jersey)
Speaker & Educator — university outreach, TEDx, conferences
AI Responsible Practice Advocate — LangChain partnership, safe enterprise AI
Researcher — graph change-point detection (martingale + Shapley attribution)
DevOps / DevSecOps & Practical Security (CTFs, Hack The Box)
